message:
Improve README with detailed install instructions, feature list, and known limitations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,99 +1,116 @@
 # Mutter Butter
 
-A GNOME Shell extension that anchors windows to a configurable grid with live-scaling "postage stamp" miniatures for inactive windows.
+A GNOME Shell extension for grid-based window management with live-scaling stamps.
 
-![GNOME 50](https://img.shields.io/badge/GNOME-50-blue)
+Mutter Butter divides each monitor into a configurable grid and automatically manages window placement. When you have more windows than screen real estate, the least-recently-focused windows shrink into live "postage stamp" previews — small, continuously-rendered clones that stay visible and clickable. Click a stamp to restore it; the lowest-ranked full-size window takes its place.
 
-## What it does
+## Features
 
-Mutter Butter replaces GNOME's default window placement with a grid-based system where:
+- **Grid-based placement** — windows snap to a per-monitor grid (default 8x6) on move or open
+- **Auto-shrink** — windows below a configurable focus-rank threshold shrink to 25% live previews
+- **Click-to-restore** — click any stamp to bring it back to full size
+- **Manual zoom** — `Super+=` / `Super+-` to step through scale levels (25%, 50%, 75%, 100%)
+- **Drag and drop** — drag stamps or full-size windows to reposition them on the grid; drop on an occupied cell to swap
+- **Hover preview** — hover over a stamp to see the real window at full size next to it
+- **Placement modes** — stamps can stay in-place, line up on a shelf edge, or pack into gaps
+- **Per-monitor grids** — independent column/row configuration for each display
+- **Exempt apps** — exclude specific applications from auto-shrink
+- **Workspace-aware** — stamps slide with workspace transitions
 
-- **Windows snap to a configurable grid** — per-monitor NxM grid with drag-and-drop reflow
-- **Inactive windows auto-shrink to postage stamps** — live-rendered miniatures via `Clutter.Clone`, no content reflow
-- **Stamps are interactive** — click to restore, hover to preview at full size, drag to reposition
-- **Manual zoom** — scale any window through discrete steps with `Super+=` / `Super+-`
-- **Three placement modes** — in-place (shrink where they sit), shelf (dock to an edge), or pack (compact arrangement)
-- **Per-app exemptions** — keep certain apps full-size regardless of focus rank
+## Requirements
 
-The result is a persistent, live Exposé where windows coexist at different scale factors.
+- GNOME Shell 50
+- GJS (for running unit tests)
 
-## Installation
+## Install
 
 ### From source
 
 ```bash
 git clone https://github.com/obra/mutter-butter.git
 cd mutter-butter
-glib-compile-schemas schemas/
-cp -r . ~/.local/share/gnome-shell/extensions/mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io
+
+# Package and install
+zip -r /tmp/mutter-butter.zip metadata.json extension.js prefs.js stylesheet.css lib/ schemas/ \
+    -x "schemas/gschemas.compiled"
+gnome-extensions install /tmp/mutter-butter.zip --force
+
+# Compile schemas
+glib-compile-schemas ~/.local/share/gnome-shell/extensions/mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io/schemas/
+```
+
+Log out and back in (Wayland requires a session restart for new extensions), then enable:
+
+```bash
+gnome-extensions enable mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io
 ```
 
-Then restart GNOME Shell (`Alt+F2`, type `r`, press Enter on X11 — or log out and back in on Wayland) and enable the extension:
+### Update after code changes
 
 ```bash
+zip -r /tmp/mutter-butter.zip metadata.json extension.js prefs.js stylesheet.css lib/ schemas/ \
+    -x "schemas/gschemas.compiled"
+gnome-extensions install /tmp/mutter-butter.zip --force
+glib-compile-schemas ~/.local/share/gnome-shell/extensions/mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io/schemas/
+gnome-extensions disable mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io
 gnome-extensions enable mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io
 ```
 
 ## Configuration
 
-Open the extension preferences via GNOME Extensions app or:
+Open preferences with:
 
 ```bash
 gnome-extensions prefs mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io
 ```
 
-Settings include:
+**Grid** — per-monitor column and row counts
 
-| Setting | Default | Description |
-|---|---|---|
-| Grid columns | 8 | Number of grid columns (per monitor) |
-| Grid rows | 6 | Number of grid rows (per monitor) |
-| Auto-shrink | On | Shrink windows below the focus rank threshold |
-| Focus rank threshold | 3 | Number of top-ranked windows that stay full size |
-| Placement mode | in-place | How stamps are positioned: in-place, shelf, or pack |
-| Shelf edge | bottom | Which screen edge for shelf mode |
-| Hysteresis | 2000ms | Cooldown before a just-shrunk window can be indirectly restored |
-
-## Keyboard shortcuts
-
-| Shortcut | Action |
-|---|---|
-| `Super+=` | Zoom in (increase scale) |
-| `Super+-` | Zoom out (decrease scale) |
+**Behavior:**
+- Auto-shrink on/off and focus rank threshold
+- Stamp placement mode (in-place, shelf, pack)
+- Shelf edge and depth
+- Hysteresis cooldown for indirect restoration
+- Zoom keybindings
+- Exempt application list
 
 ## Architecture
 
-Seven internal modules with a clean separation between testable pure logic and Shell-dependent code:
+The extension separates pure logic from Shell-dependent code:
+
+**Pure-logic modules** (in `lib/`, testable standalone):
+- `gridEngine` — cell math, placement, occupancy tracking
+- `stampPlacer` — in-place, shelf, and pack placement strategies
+- `focusTracker` — MRU ranking with threshold queries
 
-| Module | Responsibility |
-|---|---|
-| `gridEngine` | Per-monitor grid math, cell allocation, window-to-cell mapping |
-| `scaleManager` | Clone lifecycle, allocation-based scaling, per-window state |
-| `focusTracker` | Ordered focus-rank list, auto-shrink/restore with hysteresis |
-| `stampPlacer` | Stamp placement strategies (in-place, shelf, pack) |
-| `dragHandler` | Intercepts native grab ops and stamp drag, grid snapping |
-| `inputRouter` | Clone containers, pick visibility, click/hover handling |
-| `windowStateHandler` | Fullscreen, maximized, tiled, minimized state management |
+**Shell-dependent modules** (in `lib/`, tested via nested Mutter):
+- `scaleManager` — clone lifecycle, opacity/pick management, auto-shrink/restore
+- `inputRouter` — stamp click/drag detection, z-ordering
+- `dragHandler` — grab-op interception, grid overlay, snap-on-drop
+- `windowStateHandler` — fullscreen, maximized, tiled, minimized transitions
 
-See [the design spec](docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-03-mutter-butter-design.md) for the full technical design.
+See [`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-03-mutter-butter-design.md`](docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-03-mutter-butter-design.md) for the full design document.
 
 ## Testing
 
+### Unit tests
+
 ```bash
-# Unit tests (pure-logic modules, no GNOME Shell required)
 gjs -m tests/gridEngine.test.js
 gjs -m tests/stampPlacer.test.js
 gjs -m tests/focusTracker.test.js
-
-# Integration testing
-mutter --nested --wayland  # then load the extension in the nested session
 ```
 
-## Requirements
+### Integration testing
 
-- GNOME Shell 50
-- GJS (for running tests outside Shell)
+See [`docs/TESTING.md`](docs/TESTING.md) for the full testing guide, including a live testing checklist and headless CI setup.
+
+## Known limitations
+
+- `Clutter.Clone` may show black rectangles for video players or GL-accelerated content (DMA-BUF subsurface limitation — same as GNOME's Overview)
+- Grid dimension changes in preferences take effect on extension disable/enable, not immediately
+- Exemption is per-app, not per-window
 
 ## License
 
-MIT — see [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.
+[MIT](LICENSE)
